algae_description: White/pale fuzzy growth coating a dark vertical structure (likely a sponge filter or filter column) — possibly bacterial biofilm or fungal fuzz rather than true algae, given the white coloration. Consistent with cycling tank biofilm.
concerns: White fuzzy coating on filter/hardscape — likely bacterial biofilm or fungal growth consistent with an active nitrogen cycle, but worth monitoring. If it persists or spreads post-cycle, may warrant a closer look.
Image is underexposed and taken from a side/back angle, limiting full tank assessment.
narrative: A close-up shot of what appears to be a sponge filter or filter column running vertically through the tank, heavily coated in white fuzzy biofilm — classic nitrogen cycle funk. One shrimp is visible mid-frame, small and pale, appearing to graze or explore the biofilm-covered surface, which is exactly the kind of foraging behavior you'd hope to see. The backlighting blows out the water column, but what's visible of the water itself looks clear.
